title: Fix SSR hydration mismatch: stable IDs for UInput in admin forms
description:
Fix Vue SSR hydration mismatch warnings caused by UInput generating random IDs (like 'input-i2qtifm') that differ between SSR and client renders.

The error: '[Vue warn]: Hydration attribute mismatch on JSHandle@node: rendered on server: id="input-i2qtifm", expected on client: id="input-a738lt2"'

Root cause: UInput (in components/U/UInput.vue) generates random IDs internally using Math.random() or similar. When SSR renders with one ID and client hydrates with another, Vue warns.

Fix: In components/U/UInput.vue, use Vue 3's `useId()` composable (available in Vue 3.5+) to generate stable, deterministic IDs that are consistent between SSR and client. Replace the current random ID generation with `const inputId = useId()`.

If useId() is not available (Vue < 3.5), use a prop fallback: accept an optional `id` prop and use it when provided, falling back to a computed stable ID based on component label.

Also check components/U/UTextarea.vue for the same issue (UTextarea also shows this warning) and apply the same fix.

Do NOT change any page files or store files — only touch the U/UInput.vue and U/UTextarea.vue components.

Verify with: cd frontend && npm run type-check
acceptance_criteria: UInput.vue uses useId() or stable ID generation
UTextarea.vue uses useId() or stable ID generation
No 'Hydration attribute mismatch' warnings for input IDs
